scenario: Executable Gradle wrapper changes a no-primary-package-manager report into local validation guidance
fixtureState: Synthetic Gradle project with executable gradlew, settings.gradle.kts, build.gradle.kts, and a documented ./gradlew test validation claim.
taskSummary: Choose the first validation command for a repository with Gradle wrapper facts instead of stopping at generic read-only inspection.
relevantHabitatSignals: Repository fact: executable gradlew is present.
Repository facts include settings.gradle.kts and build.gradle.kts.
Generated context selects the Gradle wrapper and prefers ./gradlew test before broad exploration.

prefer: using the project-local wrapper instead of a global Gradle assumption
askFirst: running dependency mutation commands before reading policy
doNot: run global package installs to make Gradle available
quote raw instruction prose in generated artifacts
reviewFirst: scan_result.json if a machine consumer needs sanitized validation claim metadata

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Treat the repository as having no primary package-manager signal and continue broad read-only inspection.
withoutHabitatContext.risk: The agent can miss the project-local wrapper and delay validation behind generic exploration.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use the executable Gradle wrapper as the local validation entry point.

evidenceNormalizationImplication: The slice justifies a bounded Gradle wrapper project fact, not broad Android ecosystem coverage.

verdict.reason: Habitat changed the next command from generic inspection to the project-local Gradle wrapper validation path while preserving raw-prose privacy.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Only add deeper Gradle evidence if repeated observations need wrapper-version, task-list, or Android-specific command constraints.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If agents still miss executable Gradle wrappers, add a short-context Fact line or representative generated-output fixture.
